name: Barley
summary: A chewy whole grain rich in the same beta-glucan fiber as oats — good for blood sugar and heart health.

benefits.claim: Beta-glucan fiber for steadier blood sugar
benefits.mechanism: Barley is one of the richest sources of beta-glucan, a soluble fiber that thickens gut contents and slows carbohydrate absorption, flattening the post-meal glucose rise. Reviews of dietary fibre and whole grains show improved glycemic control.

citations.title: Dietary fibre and whole grains in diabetes management: systematic review and meta-analyses (Reynolds et al., PLoS Med 2020)

benefits.claim: A whole grain for heart health
benefits.mechanism: As a fiber-rich whole grain, barley fits the dietary pattern repeatedly linked to lower rates of heart disease and premature death — its beta-glucan also helps lower LDL cholesterol, much like oats.

citations.title: Carbohydrate quality and human health: a series of systematic reviews and meta-analyses (Reynolds et al., Lancet 2019)

surprisingFacts.text: Common 'pearl' barley has been polished to remove the bran, so it cooks faster but loses fiber — hulled or hull-less barley keeps the whole grain and its beta-glucan.
